meta.title: IntelliFlow CRM — N+1 Query Audit Findings
meta.generatedFrom: Multi-agent audit workflow (61 agents, 14 bounded contexts + inventory + adversarial verification + completeness critic)

severityModel.Critical: Query count scales with tenant/customer/user/record count on a user-facing request/server-action/tRPC path.
severityModel.High: Prisma query inside a loop/map over an unbounded collection (any execution context, incl. background jobs).
severityModel.Medium: Repeated relation lookup that can be batched but is currently bounded by a small fixed/paginated/Zod-capped collection.
severityModel.Low: Suspicious or bounded-fixed-count pattern; cleanup opportunity, negligible production risk; or non-Prisma port loop.

meta.note: severity is POST-VERIFICATION (adversarial verdict overrides the raw scanner severity where a verdict exists). 'verified' indicates an explicit adversarial verdict was produced. Critical+High findings were all adversarially verified; Medium/Low retain scanner severity unless noted.

findings.entryPoint: tRPC experiment.list (experiment.router.ts:211) — tenantProcedure query, no pagination
findings.problem: experiment.findMany({where:{tenantId}}) with no take, then per-experiment: countByVariant('control') + countByVariant('treatment') + resultRepo.findByExperimentId = 3N+1 queries on every experiments-list page load.
findings.fix: After findMany, collect experimentIds; one experimentAssignment.groupBy({by:['experimentId','variant'],where:{experimentId:{in:ids}},_count:true}) + one experimentResult.findMany({where:{experimentId:{in:ids}}}); merge via Maps in memory. Collapses 3N+1 → 3.

findings.entryPoint: tRPC lead.bulkScore (lead.router.ts:1232) — input z.array(idSchema) with NO .max() cap
findings.problem: bulkScoreLeads loops leadIds serially: each iteration = findById (read) + external AI call + save (write). 2N DB + N AI, unbounded — also a DoS vector via uncapped array.
findings.fix: Add .max() to the Zod input. Batch findMany({where:{id:{in:dedupedIds}}}); run AI scoring with a concurrency-limited Promise pool; persist via one $transaction/updateMany. Return per-id verdicts from the batched result.

findings.entryPoint: tRPC feedback.exportTrainingData (feedback.router.ts:116) — date-range driven, no LIMIT
findings.problem: Loops trainingCorrections (findByModelVersion over a caller-supplied date range, unbounded), calling leadDataPort.getLeadData(leadId) per element — one extra DB round-trip per correction.
findings.fix: Add LeadDataPort.getLeadDataBatch(ids): Promise<Map>; collect distinct leadIds; single batched fetch; resolve from Map in the loop. Adapter implements as findMany({where:{id:{in:ids}}}).

findings.entryPoint: processExpiredDrafts (scheduler). findExpired is a bare findMany (no take) filtered by tenantId+expiresAt+status.
findings.problem: For N expired drafts: N save() UPDATEs + N publishEvents, serial. Per-run cost grows linearly with tenant draft volume.
findings.fix: Add draftRepository.expireDraftsBeforeDate(tenantId, now) → single updateMany({where:{tenantId,expiresAt:{lt:now},status:{notIn:terminal}},data:{status:'EXPIRED'}}). Publish one bulk expiry event, or keep per-draft events in memory but persist with one updateMany.

findings.entryPoint: dispatchScheduledInsights. activeTenants = lead.groupBy(by:tenantId) — one row per active tenant, no cap.
findings.problem: Per tenant: user.findFirst(role:ADMIN) + fallback findFirst. O(N tenants) serial round-trips per scheduler run.
findings.fix: One user.findMany({where:{tenantId:{in:tenantIds},role:'ADMIN'},distinct:['tenantId']}); build Map<tenantId,userId>; batched fallback findMany for tenants with no ADMIN.

findings.problem: Per tenant ≈6 DB round-trips (4 heuristic queries + admin lookups), serial across unbounded active-tenant list.
findings.fix: Per-tenant fan-out is architecturally motivated; mitigate by BullMQ addBulk for queue.add, and bulk-fetch settings/admin data before the loop. For large tenant counts, restructure heuristics into bulk SQL keyed by tenantId IN.

findings.problem: settings = ticketAutomationSetting.findMany({}) (sweepAll → all tenants). Per tenant: ticket.findMany(take:500). 201 queries / 5 min at 200 tenants.
findings.fix: When sweepAll: one ticket.findMany({where:{tenantId:{in:settings.map(s=>s.tenantId)},...}}); group by tenantId in memory (Map.groupBy); iterate settings against pre-grouped results.

findings.problem: SLA-breach sweep: per ticket update(slaStatus) + conditional notification.create. 2N round-trips.
findings.fix: ticket.updateMany({where:{id:{in:breachedIds}},data:{slaStatus:'BREACHED'}}) + notification.createMany. 2N → 2.

findings.problem: SLA-warning sweep: same 2N pattern flipping slaStatus='AT_RISK'.
findings.fix: updateMany + createMany, same as NP-008.

findings.problem: Per-tenant case.findMany(take:500), unbounded tenant loop (not covered by the ticket-job globs).
findings.fix: Single case.findMany with tenantId IN (or no filter, take:500), group by tenantId in memory; batch the inner case.update via updateMany.

findings.problem: Inner loop: handleOverdueCase issues case.update per case + per-case notification.
findings.fix: Collect overdue IDs + next-priority, batch updateMany after the scan; accumulate notification payloads, flush via createMany.

findings.problem: 2 DB calls per id; capped at 100 → worst case 200 round-trips.
findings.fix: findMany({where:{id:{in:ids},tenantId},include:{_count:{opportunities}}}) → Map; deleteMany({where:{id:{in:eligibleIds},tenantId}}); collect failed ids from the prefetch map.

findings.problem: 2-3 serial DB calls per id; capped 100 → up to 300 round-trips.
findings.fix: findMany requested accounts once; verify target user once; single updateMany({where:{id:{in:validIds},tenantId},data:{ownerId}}); emit notifications via Promise.allSettled.

findings.problem: findById per id, capped 100. (bulkArchive:760 and bulkDelete:806 share the shape → NP-037/NP-038.)
findings.fix: Add PrismaCaseDocumentRepository.findManyById(ids) → findMany({where:{id:{in:ids},tenantId,deletedAt:null},include:{acl:true}}); build Map; iterate ids against map.

findings.problem: 3 sequential for-loops (deals/leads/contacts); getAllInsights passes take:50 each → up to 150 serial dedup-transactions per request (non-blocking, but real DB load).
findings.fix: Collect all (sourceType,sourceId) pairs; one findMany to fetch existing active notifications → Set; createMany the missing ones. Collapses O(N) tx → 1 findMany + 1 createMany.

findings.problem: Same dedup-per-element pattern for hotLeads (NP-041 covers the staleContacts arm).
findings.fix: Shared batch with NP-015.

findings.problem: Per-tenant settings findUnique inside a tenant loop sourced from account.groupBy(by:tenantId).
findings.fix: Hoist: accountAutomationSetting.findMany({where:{tenantId:{in:tenantIds},aiAccountScoring:true}}) → Set; membership check in loop.

findings.problem: 2N settings lookups across unbounded tenant list.
findings.fix: Two batched findMany before the loop → two Sets; membership checks inside.

findings.problem: Up to 200 serial round-trips per tick (save + audit per item).
findings.fix: Batch status reset via updateMany; concurrency-limited Promise.all for delivery; accumulate audit rows → createMany.

findings.problem: findAllVersions walks parentVersionId chain, 1 findUnique per hop; no depth cap.
findings.fix: Single recursive CTE via $queryRaw (parameterised) to collect chain ids, then one findMany({where:{id:{in:ids}}}).

findings.problem: findVersionChain BFS, 2 queries per node. Latent: becomes Critical the moment it is wired to a route.
findings.fix: Same recursive-CTE collect-then-findMany as NP-020.

findings.executionContext: unreachable (no production caller — no bulkComplete tRPC procedure)
findings.problem: bulkComplete 2N serial; latent until wired.
findings.fix: Batch findMany({where:{id:{in:ids}}}); apply complete() in memory; persist via one $transaction/updateMany.

findings.problem: Sequential across metrics; parallel only within each metric's months.
findings.fix: Flatten: Promise.all(metrics.flatMap(m => monthRanges.map(r => getMetricValue(...).then(...)))); group back by metric.

findings.problem: Bulk stage-change fires N separate tRPC mutations → N HTTP + N Prisma writes.
findings.fix: Add tRPC opportunity.bulkUpdateStage({ids,stage}) → updateMany. NOTE: API contract ADDITION (see ADR / ask).

findings.problem: Bulk delete fires N mutations.
findings.fix: Add tRPC opportunity.bulkDelete({ids}) → deleteMany({where:{id:{in:ids},tenantId}}). API contract ADDITION.

findings.problem: N concurrent upserts per save (one round-trip per field).
findings.fix: deleteMany({where:{tenantId}}) + createMany — constant 2 statements (full-replacement semantics already intended).
deleteMany + createMany inside the same transaction (pattern already used by duplicateRules.update in this file).
deleteMany({where:{tenantId}}) + createMany.
Pre-load recent reminders (findMany WHERE sourceId IN, createdAt>=cutoff) → Set; filter; single createMany.
Dedup-then-batch (findMany → Set → createMany).
Dedup-then-batch.
Dedup-then-batch.

findings.problem: Per-tenant retention sweep runs serially; per-tenant updateMany/deleteMany cannot be merged (tenant-scoped).
findings.fix: Fan out with concurrency-limited Promise.all (p-limit) instead of serial for-of.

findings.problem: findByRollbackToken fetches ALL approved actions for a tenant then scans in JS for a token in JSON. Not classic N+1 but unbounded memory/scan.
findings.fix: Add a generated column/partial index on proposedState->>'rollbackToken' and query it directly (parameterised $queryRaw); interim: add take:500 to bound the scan.

findings.problem: validateEntityReferences does up to 3 serial findUnique on the hot create/update path (not scaling, but needlessly serial).
findings.fix: Wrap the 3 conditional lookups in a single Promise.all.

findings.problem: Serial upsert loop inside a transaction (also at :198). Practically ~7/6 items but no hard cap.
findings.fix: deleteMany + createMany (the updateMany already deactivates all first); add .max() to the schema.
findManyById + in-memory archive() + single updateMany (status='ARCHIVED').
findManyById + single updateMany (deletedAt). Keep per-doc legal-hold check in memory before the batch write.
Accumulate payloads → Promise.allSettled / createMany.
Same batch approach as NP-019 (processRetries sibling).
Folded into the NP-015 batch.

findings.note: Includes persistInsightNotification:624 (verdict: not-an-issue) and persistEntityActivity leadActivity:693.
findings.fix: Collect titles/entityIds; one findMany dedup → Set; createMany non-duplicates. Lower priority (bounded ~30).

findings.problem: 8 sequential COUNT queries (fixed arity — not scaling N+1, but a cleanup).
findings.fix: Single groupBy({by:['status'],where:{tenantId},_count:{status:true}}); default missing statuses to 0.

findings.note: Also lines 133/179 (search) and 1010 (stats). getStats already optimised to UNION ALL.
findings.problem: Not data-scaling N+1; concern is 7 parallel connections per request under load.
findings.fix: Connection-pool-aware concurrency cap (p-limit(4)); optionally extend UNION ALL $queryRaw to searchFeed.
findMany({where:{id:{in:ids},tenantId}}) → Map, pair with similarity.
Collapse into one findFirst with relation filter email:{tenantId}.

findings.problem: 5 parallel queries; the inbox arm loads all unread metadata into memory for JS filtering (degrades as inbox grows).
findings.fix: GROUP BY query for folder counts; DB-side NOT filter for inbox instead of in-memory filtering.
Promise.all within the tx, or deleteMany+createMany (resetToDefaults path).

findings.problem: getTenantPlan loads ALL tenant user ids into memory to feed an IN filter; 2 queries on every module-entitlement check. Not N+1 but avoidable.
findings.fix: Single relation-traversal: workspace.findFirst({where:{members:{some:{user:{tenantId}}}}}).
$executeRaw UPSERT ... ON CONFLICT with unnest(), or wrap in $transaction; low priority.

findings.problem: Promise.all of individual creates (each does validateTenant + create) instead of one createMany.
findings.fix: Validate distinct tenantIds in one findMany; single auditLogEntry.createMany.

findings.note: Also :167. Port call, NOT Prisma — lower impact.
findings.fix: Promise.all over cancellations; add cancelScheduledBatch to the port if downstream supports it.

findings.note: Also :508. Pagination loop is intentional for memory-bounded reindex.
findings.fix: Switch offset to cursor-based pagination to avoid skip-scan cliff on large tables. No N+1 elimination needed.

findings.problem: Idempotency check per event; collection is one aggregate's domain events (small constant), so not data-scaling.
findings.fix: Optional: batch idempotency findMany + createMany(skipDuplicates). Low priority.
Pass calendarId into the initial create/upsert; remove the second update.
deleteMany + createMany replace pattern (consistent with NP-026..NP-028).

verifiedFalsePositives.reason: Single findMany + in-memory .filter()/.reduce() — exactly 3 DB calls regardless of N. NOT N+1. (Separate memory smell: unbounded findMany of all scores; consider aggregate.)
buckets come from pure in-memory getDateBuckets(); router caps day≤31/week≤52; bounded parallel time-series fan-out, not N+1.
Fixed 2-count Promise.all (won/lost); constant width, not a loop.
filterStaleInsights already batches one findMany per entity-type (≤5). Optimal.
topPerformers groupBy(take:5) then findMany(id in) — already the correct batch pattern.
resolveTenant iterates one email's To-recipients (small, RFC-bounded) with early return on first match. Not data-scaling.
saveAll is an intentional bulk-write (attendees/cases already use createMany); no production callers; not a read N+1.

rawSql.note: No new unsafe raw SQL is introduced by this audit. Existing $queryRawUnsafe sites to be aware of (allowlist-guarded but interpolated):

sites.detail: SET app.current_tenant_id = '${tenantId}' — interpolates a UUID; recommend SET LOCAL with a bound parameter / set_config($1,$2,true).
granularity interpolated after Set allowlist; tenantId/from/to are positional params.

rawSql.remediationRawSqlPolicy: Any recursive-CTE fix (NP-020/NP-021/account ancestors) MUST use Prisma.sql tagged templates / parameterised $queryRaw, be covered by tests, and be flagged for review.
